summary: Comprehensive testing of the new MULTI-METHOD payments framework. Backend: 12/12 tests pass (test_payments_multi.py). Frontend: full E2E verified — Settings toggles for 4 methods, 3/3 cap enforced (4th toggle no-op), per-method fields (paypal_me URL / bank name+sort+number) persisted via PUT /settings; server-side cap to 3 confirmed; booking payment_status matrix (no-methods=not_required, only_in_person=pay_in_person, >=1 online=pending) verified; PaymentPanel renders all 3 enabled methods (paypal_me link with /{amount}GBP suffix, bank details with reference, in-person message); notify-paid button sets deposit_claimed=true while status stays pending; pay-in-person link switches to pay_in_person; PayPal-only unconfigured shows fallback message; regression with no methods produces zero payment UI. State fully restored (methods=[], deposits=0, bank/paypalme fields cleared).
critical_code_review_comments: Settings.jsx has two Save buttons (save-settings and save-payment-settings) — both PUT full settings; behaviour is correct but repetition is a minor UX concern.

PaymentPanel.jsx: when methods=['paypal'] and paypal is NOT configured, the fallback text is only rendered if methods.length===1. If paypal is combined with only in_person (methods.length>1) and neither can render, the UI could look sparse — currently paired with paypal_me/bank_transfer in-person so not a real issue today.
Server: PUT /settings caps payment_methods to first 3 but silently drops extras — consider returning a 400 or warning for clearer feedback (functional today).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all critical flows: settings, cap, save, booking matrix, PaymentPanel rendering, notify-paid, pay-in-person, PayPal fallback, no-methods regression)

seed_data_creation: Several TEST_ bookings created (TEST_MultiUI, TEST_PPOnly, TEST_NoMethods and pytest bookings). All emails contain 'example.com' for easy cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Settings restored to default state after tests: payment_methods=[], payment_method='off', paypal_me_url='', bank fields cleared, all shop deposit_amount=0 and deposit_required=false. PayPal card checkout endpoint intentionally NOT configured — 400 'not configured' is EXPECTED. Reusable pytest: /app/backend/tests/test_payments_multi.py (12 tests). Older /app/backend/tests/test_payments.py still exists but tests the legacy single-method API; may be superseded by test_payments_multi.py.

verified_behaviours.GET /api/payments/config: Returns {methods:[], method:'off'|first, paypal_me_url, bank:{account_name,sort_code,account_number}, currency, paypal_client_id, paypal_configured}. Verified.
verified_behaviours.PUT /api/settings caps to 3: Sending 4 methods persists only first 3 (verified via test_server_caps_to_three).
verified_behaviours.Client-side 3 cap: 4th toggle click does nothing; counter shows 3/3 SELECTED, paypal row border remains var(--line) (not gold).
verified_behaviours.Booking status matrix: no methods -> not_required (deposit forced to 0); only in_person -> pay_in_person; paypal_me+in_person -> pending. All verified with real bookings.
verified_behaviours.PaymentPanel multi-method render: £25.00 amount; paypalme-link href=https://paypal.me/testshop/25.00GBP; bank block shows Name/Sort code/Account no/Reference; in_person message.
verified_behaviours.POST /public/bookings/{ref}/notify-paid: Returns 200; deposit_claimed=true; payment_status stays 'pending'; UI shows deposit-claimed message.
verified_behaviours.PayPal card unconfigured fallback: With methods=['paypal'] only, PaymentPanel shows 'Online payment is not yet available…' fallback; method-paypal component not rendered (no client keys).
verified_behaviours.Regression (no methods): Booking created with methods=[] has payment_status='not_required' and deposit_amount=0; PaymentPanel returns null (no payment UI); reschedule/cancel buttons still visible.
